## Cases mentioned in passing

### Panama Canal
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "Panama Canal"
- **Anchor:** `§3.p2`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "The Erie Canal — we talked about the Erie Canal. This is the building of the Erie Canal, and it talks about Benjamin Wright. He was the first civil engineer in the country, and for every civil engineer of the next 50 years, he was the engineer for the Erie Canal. He knew nothing about engineering when he was given the job of building the Erie Canal, but he learned very quickly. The Panama Canal is considered one of the greatest achievements of the last hundred years and more. This is a Greek canal, and the cliffs are sheer — they just cut straight through the rock."

### BU Management School minority fellowship program
- **canonical_cluster_id:** "BU Management School minority fellowship program"
- **Anchor:** `§9.p2`
- **Quote (layer 3):** "I'll tell you a story about something similar that happened over at BU in the management school. Some donor to the management school wanted to have more minorities, so he gave 10 full-ride scholarships to go to management school. Ordinarily you pay your own way through management school. They gave out 10 of these fellowships the first year to minority students, and not half made it through. Rather than saying, well, minorities just can't hack it, they said, wait, this is our fault. We thought we would bring them in here and just throw them in with everybody else. So the next year they..."
